refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a key performance indicator (KPI) requirement that directly impacts customer productivity and system acceptance. Refinement adds explicit reference to measurement/monitoring (telemetry) and duty cycle context to make verification concrete. High safety criticality tailoring requires that cycle time optimization does not compromise safety interlocks or emergency response.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Instrumented field trials under representative duty cycle; p95 cycle time computed from telemetry logs; comparison against CT_target acceptance threshold; safety event correlation analysis to ensure no safety compromise.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-002.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within design envelope.
FR-NGHE-002.2: The teleoperation system shall introduce latency and control overhead not exceeding allocated cycle time budget.
FR-NGHE-002.3: Safety services shall not introduce blocking delays that degrade cycle time below target, except during safety-critical interventions.
FR-NGHE-002.4: The NGHE solution shall continuously monitor and report cycle time metrics via telemetry for performance tracking.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Optimize vehicle hydraulic/mechanical actuation timing; minimize dwell states; tune operator control responsiveness.
DP-B: Implement predictive cycle scheduling with pre-positioning and parallel task execution (load while traveling).
DP-C: Deploy low-latency teleoperation network with edge compute; reduce round-trip control delay.
DP-D: Integrate safety services as non-blocking asynchronous monitors; use safety-aware task scheduling to avoid safety-induced stalls.
DP-E: Establish real-time telemetry pipeline with cycle time KPI aggregation and anomaly detection.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-Integrated: Combine DP-A (vehicle actuation optimization), DP-D (non-blocking safety integration), and DP-E (telemetry-driven performance monitoring). Vehicle cycle timing is the primary lever; safety services must be architected to avoid blocking delays; telemetry provides closed-loop visibility for continuous improvement.

potential_sources: Vehicle actuation timing (DP-A) is decoupled from safety service logic (DP-D) via asynchronous monitoring and non-blocking safety state machine.
Telemetry pipeline (DP-E) is a passive observer; does not couple to vehicle or safety control paths.
Teleoperation latency (DP-C) is a separate concern; addressed at sub-FR level.
coupling_check.notes: The integrated DP respects the independence axiom: vehicle performance, safety assurance, and observability are logically separable. Risk: safety-critical interventions (e.g., emergency stop) must still be synchronous; this is a design constraint, not a coupling violation.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: CT_target is achievable within vehicle kinematic/hydraulic limits and site conditions (e.g., ground bearing, slope).
Assumption: Teleoperation latency budget is compatible with operator control loop stability.
Risk: Safety interlocks (e.g., load stability checks, proximity alerts) may introduce latency; must be quantified and allocated.
Risk: Duty cycle variability (soil type, load mass, operator skill) may cause p95 to exceed target; requires statistical validation.
Risk: Network congestion or telemetry backlog could mask real cycle time degradation.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Decompose DP-Integrated into explicit sub-DPs for each sub-FR; quantify latency budgets for safety services and teleoperation; establish cycle time measurement baseline before optimization.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: The Vehicle module owns the mechanical, hydraulic, and electrical actuation systems that execute the load→travel→dump cycle. It is the primary realization of cycle time performance. The Vehicle module interfaces with Teleoperation for operator commands and with Safety Services for safety-critical state validation.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Operator commands (load, travel, dump) from Teleoperation or autonomous task scheduler; drives vehicle actuation sequencing.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Safety state signals (e.g., load stability, proximity warnings, emergency stop) from Safety Services; Vehicle must respect safety constraints before executing motion.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle sensor data (position, velocity, load mass, cycle timestamps, actuation state) streamed to NGHE Solution for KPI aggregation and monitoring.

module_containment_scores.notes: Vehicle is the natural owner of actuation timing (DP-A) and cycle execution logic. Cycle time is primarily a vehicle performance attribute. Minor diffusion: telemetry timestamp generation requires coordination with NGHE Solution time sync.

Teleoperation owns operator control interface and network latency (DP-C), but does not own vehicle actuation or cycle timing. High diffusion: cycle time depends on vehicle response, not teleoperation logic alone.

Safety Services owns safety-aware task scheduling (DP-D) to avoid blocking delays, but does not own vehicle actuation. Diffusion: safety constraints are enforced at Vehicle level; Safety Services provides state signals only.

NGHE Solution owns telemetry aggregation and KPI reporting (DP-E), but cycle time performance is realized in Vehicle. Moderate containment: NGHE Solution is the system-level observer and performance tracker.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Vehicle module is the primary owner of cycle time performance. It realizes DP-A (actuation timing optimization) and executes the load→travel→dump cycle. Safety Services and Teleoperation provide inputs (SafetyAlert, Tasking) that constrain or influence cycle timing, but do not own the cycle itself. NGHE Solution provides observability (Telemetry) and KPI aggregation.
containment_risks: Risk: If safety-critical latency is not properly budgeted in Vehicle design, safety interlocks could become a bottleneck, degrading cycle time below target.
Risk: If teleoperation latency is high, operator responsiveness may degrade cycle time; this is a Teleoperation concern, not Vehicle, but impacts overall KPI.
Risk: If telemetry pipeline is lossy or delayed, reported cycle time metrics may not reflect true performance; NGHE Solution must ensure data integrity.
Risk: Duty cycle variability (soil, load, operator) may cause Vehicle to achieve target in lab but fail in field; requires field validation.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Establish explicit cycle time budget allocation: Vehicle actuation (e.g., 80% of target), Safety Services latency (e.g., 10%), Teleoperation latency (e.g., 10%). Validate that Vehicle design can meet its allocated budget under worst-case duty cycle. Ensure telemetry timestamps are synchronized across modules for accurate p95 computation.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (integrated vehicle optimization + non-blocking safety + telemetry monitoring) directly addresses the FR (achieve p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target). The DP is logically sound: vehicle actuation is the primary performance lever, safety is decoupled via asynchronous monitoring, and telemetry provides closed-loop feedback. No functional gap identified.

diffusion_risks: DP-A (vehicle actuation timing) is well-contained in Vehicle module; low diffusion risk.
DP-D (non-blocking safety scheduling) is split between Safety Services (state generation) and Vehicle (state consumption); acceptable diffusion if interface (SafetyAlert) is well-defined.
DP-E (telemetry KPI aggregation) is split between Vehicle (data generation) and NGHE Solution (aggregation); acceptable diffusion if Telemetry interface is standardized.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle is the clear owner of cycle time realization. Safety Services and NGHE Solution are supporting modules that provide constraints and observability, respectively. No module duplication or orphaned logic detected. Interface boundaries (Tasking, SafetyAlert, Telemetry) are clear and non-overlapping.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Cycle time is directly measurable via Vehicle telemetry (timestamps for load, travel, dump sub-cycles). p95 can be computed from field trial data. Safety event logs can be correlated to ensure no safety compromise. Feasibility is high.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Vehicle owns cycle time performance; Safety Services owns safety constraint enforcement; NGHE Solution owns KPI aggregation and reporting. Allocation is clear and non-overlapping. Ownership boundaries are well-defined.

recommended_revisions: Establish quantitative cycle time budget allocation across Vehicle, Safety Services, and Teleoperation modules before detailed design.
Define SafetyAlert interface contract: which safety states block motion, which are advisory, and what are maximum latencies for each.
Define Telemetry interface contract: required timestamp precision, sampling rate, and data integrity guarantees for accurate p95 computation.
Plan field validation trials to measure p95 under representative duty cycles and site conditions; establish acceptance criteria.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-002 is a system-level KPI requirement that is not atomic. It has been successfully refined into four sub-FRs (vehicle cycle execution, teleoperation latency, safety service non-blocking design, telemetry monitoring). The selected DP is logically sound and well-allocated to the Vehicle module as primary owner. However, the requirement is complex enough that further decomposition of sub-FRs is necessary to drive detailed design and validation. Proceeding to the next level will clarify cycle time budgets, safety latency constraints, and telemetry data contracts.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-002.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within design envelope.
FR-NGHE-002.2: The teleoperation system shall introduce latency and control overhead not exceeding allocated cycle time budget.
FR-NGHE-002.3: Safety services shall not introduce blocking delays that degrade cycle time below target, except during safety-critical interventions.
FR-NGHE-002.4: The NGHE solution shall continuously monitor and report cycle time metrics via telemetry for performance tracking.

assumptions: CT_target is achievable within vehicle kinematic and hydraulic limits.
Duty cycle is well-defined and representative of field conditions.
Safety interlocks can be designed as non-blocking asynchronous monitors (except emergency stop).
Teleoperation latency is within acceptable bounds for operator control stability.
Telemetry infrastructure can provide synchronized timestamps with sufficient precision for p95 computation.
open_risks: Safety-critical latency may become a bottleneck if not properly budgeted in Vehicle design.
Duty cycle variability (soil type, load mass, operator skill) may cause field p95 to exceed lab target.
Telemetry data loss or delay could mask real cycle time degradation.
Operator skill and site conditions (slope, bearing) are external variables not fully controlled by NGHE design.
evidence_needed: Quantitative cycle time budget allocation (Vehicle, Safety Services, Teleoperation).
Safety latency analysis: which safety states block motion, maximum acceptable latencies.
Telemetry interface specification: timestamp precision, sampling rate, data integrity guarantees.
Field trial data: p95 cycle time under representative duty cycles and site conditions.
Safety event correlation analysis: confirm no safety compromise during cycle time optimization.
